data.message: Add macOS xcrun to the existing supervised validator descendant allowlists. The first canonical strict replay showed xcrun as the sole containment violation while configure, build, all 68 FAST tests, formatting, data policy, and docs checks passed; the exact replay then passed containment and all commands.
data.rationale: xcrun is the Apple toolchain dispatcher invoked by CMake and does not broaden authority beyond local build validation

data.message: Exact real replay from 0dbcd666 found 37 of 233 Heston surface derivatives outside European-call delta bounds on 2024-06-14; the fit converged but saturated the sigma upper bound. The corrected implementation records candidate/stability/reason diagnostics, nulls affected Heston hedge aggregates, and never clips invalid derivatives. Sample regression evaluates 50/50 valid rows with zero invalid; no real risk or superiority promotion is authorized.

data.message: Exact five-date OptionMetrics replay from 2ccbf543 completed with public-safe provenance. Five of five fits converged, three were boundary-saturated, and 37 of 1,212 Heston surface deltas were invalid; the aggregate gate is diagnostic_only. Heston improved median next-day IV MAE by 88.3844 bps versus Black-Scholes but was worse on median in-sample IV and price RMSE. Metrics receipt sha256 83bc58fc44f44e5214787603053bce98d9ce1a7379a113c3469459b2a7585750.

Close the claim-integrity correction honestly, then change methods: repair or replace the analytic Heston pricer/delta and boundary-saturated calibration using independent reference Greeks and robust calibration diagnostics before the next exact real-panel comparison.
data.rationale: The corrected real panel is mixed and fails both calibration and numerical validity gates; additional presentation work cannot turn it into superiority evidence.

data.goal: Establish independent Heston price and Greek references for the real OptionMetrics panel, diagnose boundary saturation and invalid deltas, and implement one scientifically justified calibration repair with claim-safe validation

data.message: Predeclare the bounded repair: remove hard-clipped optimizer coordinates and price clipping, calibrate in physical parameters with explicit stress-capable bounds and deterministic multi-start selection by the unchanged weighted IV residual objective, honor quadrature controls with a fixed high-accuracy scaled Gauss-Laguerre rule, and independently validate prices/deltas against QuantLib on a fixed grid plus aggregate-only real-panel evidence.
data.rationale: The old unconstrained-to-clipped transform creates zero-gradient plateaus; 32-node unscaled quadrature materially disagrees with QuantLib in the observed high-vol-of-vol regime; fixed direct bounds, deterministic starts, convergence-checked quadrature, and external reference errors measure pricing validity rather than headline metrics.

data.message: Accept the numerical and optimizer repair, but keep the five-date Heston superiority decision diagnostic-only because the 2024 stress fit remains boundary-saturated and its repaired price and tail-IV errors worsen.
data.rationale: The repair was predeclared before optimization and is independently reference-validated. Mixed real metrics and residual boundary saturation are direct negative evidence, so widening the bound again or promoting superiority would be post-result metric gaming.

data.message: Independent QuantLib validation passed on a fixed 27-case stress grid and all 1,239 real aggregate surface rows: zero invalid repository or reference deltas; real-panel price error median 0.000164692 dollars and p95 0.00159624; delta error median 3.92697e-7 and p95 1.03856e-5. Physical bounded three-start recalibration improved 2020-03-16 IV RMSE by 88.998% and removed all bound hits; 2024-06-14 improved IV RMSE 16.246% but remained sigma-bound-saturated with worse price/tail metrics. Aggregate receipts SHA256 0654a1a7b79352ac9403a649e6176a2735027afe3526560f50897f48ffbe8e90 and 950fdf7a2392316e9be09831201e4cd46d01cbcc8222785a011a5f4a3cdc5c16.

Strict verification and aggregate-only data policy passed.

data.goal: Implement and independently validate an arbitrage-aware SVI-family smile benchmark, then compare its aggregate calibration, price, and next-day OOS behavior with repaired Heston and Black-Scholes on existing permitted development dates without broad-panel expansion

data.message: Predeclare the bounded alternative benchmark on exactly the existing aggregate development pairs 2020-03-16 to 2020-03-17 and 2024-06-14 to 2024-06-17. Fit a power-law SSVI surface w(k,theta)=theta/2*(1+rho*phi(theta)*k+sqrt((phi(theta)*k+rho)^2+1-rho^2)), phi(theta)=eta*theta^(-gamma), with a monotone piecewise-linear ATM total-variance curve on fixed 30d/60d/90d/6m/1y knots. Use smooth parameter transforms, deterministic multistart, and the existing vega-times-quote weighted IV residual only; do not optimize headline reducers after results. Enforce sufficient SSVI wing/butterfly conditions over a fixed 20d-400d domain by construction and require independent dense calendar, density, call-monotonicity/convexity, and QuantLib Black-price checks. Compare the same in-sample IV/price and next-day OOS IV/price reducers against the repaired Heston and tenor-flat Black-Scholes baselines. Treat two-date results as development evidence only and fail closed on any arbitrage or numerical gate.
data.rationale: SSVI is a parsimonious arbitrage-aware smile surface that tests whether Heston's residual stress misspecification is model-class rather than optimizer failure. Fixed dates, knots, objective, domains, gates, and reducers prevent post-result metric selection.

data.message: The fixed two-date SSVI development benchmark completed with status development_valid. Both fitted surfaces passed sufficient SSVI wing/butterfly conditions, dense 81x401 calendar/density/call checks, finite-row gates, and 2,178 independent QuantLib prices with zero invalid values and maximum absolute price disagreement 2.83408e-13. Across 12 predeclared comparisons SSVI won 8, repaired Heston 2, and tenor-flat BS 2. Two-date median SSVI versus Heston improved IV RMSE 4.75%, IV MAE 18.51%, price RMSE 28.61%, and next-day price MAE 7.73%, while IV p90 was 3.84% worse and next-day IV MAE 0.065% worse. The 2020 next-day pair was negative for SSVI; BS won both OOS metrics. Aggregate receipt SHA256 044114ea6e4bd90eb835d6b1cf8950158d2f630d22972ba6985580c05bdcd0e4.

Accept power-law SSVI as the strongest current arbitrage-aware development baseline, preserve the repaired Heston oracle as a comparator, and keep SSVI performance superiority unpromoted until a locked larger temporal panel retains the same reducers and the negative 2020 OOS regime.
data.rationale: SSVI wins most fixed comparisons and is numerically defensible, but two dates are insufficient for general performance claims and the 2024 fit uses 96.04% of the predeclared sufficient-curvature limit.

data.summary: Implemented an arbitrage-aware power-law SSVI surface with monotone ATM variance, sufficient butterfly conditions, dense calendar/density/convexity gates, and independent QuantLib repricing. On the two fixed existing development pairs, both fits passed all gates and SSVI won 8 of 12 predeclared comparisons, including materially better median price metrics and strong 2024 next-day OOS behavior, while the negative 2020 OOS regime and structural-curvature frontier remain explicit. Strict verification and aggregate-only data policy passed.
